diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 42caf52..5f4cd82 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -310,2 +310,9 @@ Day 308 / Week 44 observations:
 - 710 new enterprise leads arrived with 440,737 seats. Sent all current system-latest threads enterprise offers.
 - Week 44 adjustment: do not start tier-12 yet. Add S2 plan credits ($10 A, $20 B/C), upgrade A to tier 3, raise quotas to A=75k/B=250k/C=750k, and raise support to global ops $100k/day plus S2 targeted ops $350k/day. Keep tier-6 capacity, low dev, and S2 content maintenance.
+
+Day 315 / Week 45 observations:
+- Retention package worked: cancellations fell to 2,758, upgrades jumped to 9,590, and subscribers recovered to 122,495. Open issues fell modestly to 7,746. Cash stayed healthy at $4.76M despite heavy support.
+- Churn is still mostly S2 A, but much lower than the prior week. Keep the package stable for another week rather than tapering too early.
+- Tier-12 costs $3.67M and would leave too little buffer with current support burn, so defer until cash builds or backlog falls.
+- 708 new enterprise leads arrived with 453,603 seats. Sent current enterprise offers. Five market research attempts discovered D_E09 Real Estate Groups and D_E02 Educational Institutions; both are enterprise groups with decent WTP. Updated the best-effort enterprise responder with D_E02/D_E09 pricing.
+- Week 45 adjustment: hold A=$59/B=$149/C=$179, S2 credits, A3/B3/C4, quotas 75k/250k/750k, tier-6 capacity, global ops $100k/day, S2 ops $350k/day, low dev, and S2 maintenance content.
